---
title: "LRP88: Mechanism model — taught receptive vocabulary (TR) -> word reading (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP88 estimates the adjusted association between taught receptive vocabulary and word
reading using *all three phase transitions* (t1$\to$t2, t2$\to$t3, t3$\to$t4) with
phase-specific intercepts — the "an additional $p$ taught spoken words is associated
with $o$ additional words read" dose-response asked for by #311 (descriptive-association
workstream #314).

::: {.callout-note title="Linear mechanism (estimand note)"}
The mechanism enters as a **linear slope** $\beta_{\text{mech}}$ on
$\text{logit}_*(TR_{\text{post}}, N_{TR})$, not the 1-D HSGP curve used elsewhere in
this family, following the vocabulary-exposure precedent (LRP56/57): the nonparametric
curve does not converge on vocabulary predictors at reporting tier, and DAG-required
adjusters are not dropped to buy convergence. The estimand is therefore the **linear**
$TR \to W$ adjusted association — a single slope, not a shape.
:::

## Adjustment set

The model conditions on this DAG-motivated adjustment set to reduce confounding of the
$TR_{\text{post}}$--$W_{\text{post}}$ association:

Parents of $TR$ under the revised DAG (2026-07-10): $\{A, GA, HS, IG, IS, RW\}$.

- $G$ — group; always conditioned on via $\beta_G$.
- $A$ — age; conditioned on linearly (a $\gamma_A\,z(\text{age})$ term).
- $HS$ — hearing status (`hs` / `hs_missing`), a common cause of the vocabulary and
  code skills; blocks every $TR \leftarrow HS \to \{\dots\} \to W$ route at its root.
- $RW$ — phonological memory (`erbto` / `erbto_missing`); blocks
  $TR \leftarrow RW \to \{TE, RV, EV, PA, NW\} \to W$.
- $W_{\text{pre}}$ — baseline word reading (temporal precedence; not a collider).
- $TR$ has no measured skill parent, so no concurrent measure confounder enters.

::: {.callout-important title="Open backdoor: intervention sessions (IS) — flagged, not adjusted"}
$IS \to TR$ and $IS \to WR$ make session dose a genuine common cause of exposure and
outcome, but $IS$ is treatment-affected ($IG \to IS$): conditioning on it would insert
a post-treatment collider between the randomised arm and latent ability
($IG \to IS \leftarrow GA \to W$). Following the family precedent and the signed-off
#309 handling of the same structure, the primary model leaves $IS$ unadjusted — so
**shared session dose is a plausible inflator of this slope**, and the estimate should
be read with that in mind. A dose-adjusted sensitivity companion is deferred.
:::

$TE$ and $RV$ are *descendants* of $TR$ that also affect $W$; conditioning on them
would block legitimate indirect paths (e.g. $TR \to TE \to W$) and pull the slope
toward the direct-only component, so they are **not** in the adjustment set. $GA$
(general ability) is latent and cannot be conditioned on; the child random intercept
proxies its time-invariant part, so the slope stays an adjusted association. Note also
that taught-vocabulary variation is largely intervention-generated: the slope
describes covariation within a treated system.

### Interpretation caveats

- *No unmeasured confounder assumption* for $(TR_{\text{post}}, W_{\text{post}})$: strong and not testable, and the flagged $IS$ backdoor is a named violation direction.
- $TR_{\text{post}}$ is measured with error; non-negligible error attenuates the slope toward zero.
- Across-phase pooling assumes the structural relationship is stable over time; a phase-specific sensitivity fit is available via the `phase_specific_mechanism` flag.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
